rationale: Both land the right top-line verdict (closed as not-reproducible), but X goes on to construct a confident, unconfirmed mechanistic theory (EOS-logit depression from 'stale captured graph buffers,' a specific repetition-attractor story, plus an oddly precise unrelated 'TODO #4923' aside) that overstates certainty the maintainer explicitly disclaimed ('I don't have a confirmed explanation... can't rule out it was a one-off fluke'). Y stays faithful to the actual closing comment — exact trial counts/commits, the corrupted-session explanation, and the GPU L20X/H200 identity note — and adds genuinely useful, low-risk artifacts (reopen checklist, isolation-findings table, capped workaround) without inventing root-cause specifics. Y's answer is marred by leftover harness boilerplate (unrelated skill-candidate listings) but that's cosmetic, not substantive, so it still wins on correctness and completeness relative to the thread's real resolution.
